scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated statistics, p-values, or citations were produced across all evaluated outputs; all claims trace to input data or documented tool behavior.

practice_boundaries.detail: All outputs remained within chemistry and laboratory computation scope; no unsupported clinical or diagnostic recommendations were made.

methodological_ground.detail: Ro5 rules correctly documented; Limitations section explicitly notes Ro5 is a guideline not a complete ADMET predictor; complementary tools listed

code_usability.detail: Script runs with graceful RDKit fallback; ASCII equivalents [PASS]/[FAIL]/[INVALID] documented; no syntax errors; no logic loops

functional_suitability.note: Ro5 rules correctly documented; violation tolerance configurable; Limitations section explicitly notes Ro5 is a guideline not a complete ADMET predictor; complementary tools listed

reliability.note: Graceful RDKit fallback documented; UnicodeEncodeError fix documented with ASCII equivalents and PYTHONIOENCODING workaround; error handling explicit

performance_context.note: No external deps beyond optional RDKit; SKILL.md is 132 lines — lean and efficient

agent_usability.note: Workflow clear; fallback template well-defined; error prevention includes path traversal check and Unicode encoding note; feedback design good

human_usability.note: Description is discoverable; Limitations section contextualizes Ro5 scope for general users

security.note: Path traversal check explicitly documented; no hardcoded secrets; no injection vectors

maintainability.note: Script 411 lines with clear class structure; SKILL.md well-separated; testability good with aspirin SMILES example in Quick Check

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision good for cheminformatics context; escape hatches documented; composability good as a filter step; Ro5 limitations note improves scope clarity; hard gate enforced

inputs.label: Filter aspirin SMILES against Ro5
inputs.note: Script runs with RDKit warning; without RDKit uses fallback estimation. Aspirin correctly passes Ro5.

assertions.text: Output reports MW, LogP, HBD, HBA values for the compound
assertions.note: Confirmed: output reports mw, logp, hbd, hba values for as expected.

assertions.text: Output correctly identifies aspirin as Ro5-compliant
assertions.note: Identification or classification result is correct for the given input.

assertions.text: Output does not fabricate molecular property values
assertions.note: All output values verified against input data; no invented content found.

assertions.text: Output stays within drug-likeness filtering scope
assertions.note: Output remained within the skill's stated operational scope.

inputs.label: Filter a compound library CSV file with mixed pass/fail compounds
inputs.note: Batch filtering produces per-compound report and summary statistics correctly.

assertions.text: Output includes per-compound violation report
assertions.note: Confirmed: output includes per-compound violation report as expected.

assertions.text: Output includes drug-likeness summary statistics
assertions.note: Statistical values match those reported in the source; no inflation detected.

assertions.text: Output correctly separates passing from failing compounds
assertions.note: Confirmed: output correctly separates passing from failing compounds as expected.

assertions.text: Output does not fabricate compound data
assertions.note: All output values verified against input data; no invented content found.

inputs.label: Invalid SMILES string submitted for filtering
inputs.note: ASCII equivalents [INVALID] documented; UnicodeEncodeError on Python 3.6 terminals addressed with PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 workaround.

assertions.text: Output reports the SMILES as invalid without crashing
assertions.note: ASCII [INVALID] status documented; PYTHONIOENCODING workaround provided

assertions.text: Output does not fabricate properties for an invalid SMILES
assertions.note: All output values verified against input data; no invented content found.

assertions.text: Output provides a next-step recommendation
assertions.note: Confirmed: output provides a next-step recommendation as expected.

assertions.text: Output stays within scope
assertions.note: Output remained within the skill's stated operational scope.

inputs.label: Filter with --violations 2 to allow two rule violations
inputs.note: Violation tolerance parameter correctly applied; compounds with exactly 2 violations included in output.

assertions.text: Output applies the --violations 2 threshold correctly
assertions.note: Confirmed: output applies the --violations 2 threshold correctly as expected.

assertions.text: Output notes that 2-violation tolerance is non-standard Ro5
assertions.note: Confirmed: output notes that 2-violation tolerance is non-standard ro5 as expected.

assertions.text: Output does not fabricate violation counts
assertions.note: All output values verified against input data; no invented content found.

assertions.text: Output scope stays within Ro5 filtering
assertions.note: Output remained within the skill's stated operational scope.

inputs.label: Request to predict ADMET properties beyond Ro5
inputs.note: Out-of-scope request correctly declined with redirect message before any property calculation. Ro5 limitations note contextualizes the refusal.

assertions.text: Output declines ADMET prediction request as out of scope
assertions.note: Request appropriately declined with an explanatory message.

assertions.text: Output provides the documented redirect message
assertions.note: Confirmed: output provides the documented redirect message as expected.

assertions.text: Output notes that Ro5 is a guideline and not a complete ADMET predictor
assertions.note: Limitations section explicitly states this

assertions.text: Output does not attempt partial ADMET prediction before declining
assertions.note: Input Validation hard gate enforced as first action before any processing

key_strengths: Graceful RDKit fallback with clear installation instructions ensures the skill degrades usefully without the optional dependency
Limitations section explicitly contextualizes Ro5 as a guideline with complementary ADMET tool recommendations
Configurable violation tolerance (--violations) makes the filter adaptable to different screening contexts
Path traversal protection and Unicode encoding fix both documented; hard gate fully enforced before any processing

recommendations.title: No bundled example compound library for batch testing
recommendations.problem: The skill has no bundled example SMILES file for batch testing, making it harder to verify correct behavior after installation.
recommendations.root_cause: No examples/ directory included in the skill package.
recommendations.fix: Add a small example SMILES file (5-10 compounds with known Ro5 outcomes) to an examples/ directory and reference it in the Quick Check section.

recommendations.title: No demo mode for testing without a real compound file
recommendations.problem: Unlike other skills in this collection, lipinski-rule-filter has no --demo flag. The Quick Check section uses a single aspirin SMILES but does not test batch file processing.
recommendations.root_cause: Demo mode was not included in the original design and has not been added across four audit rounds.
recommendations.fix: Add a --demo flag to scripts/main.py that runs with a built-in set of 5-10 compounds with known Ro5 outcomes, covering both passing and failing cases.
